content.claim: Attending a high-poverty school in Grade 8 Hungary is negatively associated with reading scores and secondary education attainment; larger direct effects on educational attainment suggest noncognitive skill and choice effects beyond test-score mechanisms.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Cross-sectional associations do not establish causation definitively
Significance of higher-education estimates is model-dependent
Causal pathway through noncognitive skills inferred rather than directly measured
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
